section1.heading: 1. General Provisions
section1.paragraph1: These Privacy Terms apply when using the web platform operated by SotsiaalAI OÜ (hereinafter SotsiaalAI or the Platform). The policy explains how SotsiaalAI collects, uses and protects personal data in line with Estonian and EU law.
section1.paragraph2: SotsiaalAI processes personal data in accordance with the Estonian Personal Data Protection Act and the EU General Data Protection Regulation.

section2.heading: 2. Data We Collect
section2.paragraph1: 2.1 Account data. Email address, role (social worker or client), and authentication method (two-step sign-in: email + one-time verification code). The one-time code is used only to confirm sign-in and is not stored permanently.
section2.paragraph2: 2.2 Subscription and payment data. Subscription type and status, payment ID and payment transaction metadata. Payments are processed by Maksekeskus AS; SotsiaalAI does not store card or bank account details.
section2.paragraph3: 2.3 Technical metadata. IP address, browser and device type, access times and log information for security, troubleshooting and service reliability purposes.

section3.heading: 3. Purposes and Legal Bases
section3.items:
• Service delivery (providing conversations, account access) - performance of a contract (GDPR Art. 6(1)(b)).
  • Payment processing - performance of a contract and legitimate interest in preventing fraud (Art. 6(1)(b),(f)).
  • Implementation of two-step authentication and account protection - performance of a contract and legitimate interest in keeping accounts secure (Art. 6(1)(b),(f)).
  • Security, error detection and reliability - legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f)).
  • Legal compliance, handling of claims and reporting - legal obligation and legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(c),(f)).

section4.heading: 4. Conversation Privacy
section4.body:
4.1 Conversation content. Conversation content (user questions, assistant replies and any other text entered) is used only to provide the service - to generate responses, mediate the conversation and show the conversation history to the user. Conversation content is not stored indefinitely, is not used for marketing, profiling or training third-party models, and is retained only for a limited time as described in section 7.

4.2 Document analysis. When using the document analysis feature, uploaded files are not stored permanently. A document is used only in the current chat window and in the context of the specific conversation. The document is removed from the system when the chat window or page is closed and is not later used for marketing, profiling or model training.

4.3 Dictation (speech recognition). Dictation mode is provided via an authorised third-party service (processor). Audio from the microphone is sent over a secure connection to the service provider, who processes it only to create text, in line with SotsiaalAI's instructions and data protection requirements. SotsiaalAI does not collect or store users' voice data permanently; audio is used only temporarily to create text and is not used for marketing or model training.

section5.heading: 5. Processors
section5.body: SotsiaalAI may use processors who handle data under SotsiaalAI's instructions, including the payment service provider (Maksekeskus AS) and the AI service provider. Contracts are in place with processors to ensure the level of data protection and confidentiality.

section6.heading: 6. Data Disclosure and Sharing
section6.body: Personal data is not shared with third parties unless required to provide the service, mandated by law or based on user consent. Payment data is processed by Maksekeskus AS under its own terms.

section7.heading: 7. Retention
section7.body:
7.1 Account data. Account data is retained until the account is deleted or upon the user's request, unless a longer retention period is required by law.

7.2 Conversation history. Conversation history shown under the ā€Conversationsā€¯ button is generally kept for up to 90 days from the last activity in the respective conversation. After that, the conversation is no longer shown to the user and is marked internally for deletion. The user can delete their conversations at any time.

7.3 Billing data. Billing and payment data are retained to the extent and for the periods required by accounting and other applicable laws.

7.4 Technical logs. Technical logs are stored for a short period for security, troubleshooting and service reliability purposes, after which they are deleted or anonymised.

section8.heading: 8. Your Rights
section8.items:
• Right to access your data and obtain a copy.
  • Right to rectify inaccurate or incomplete data.
  • Right to request erasure ("right to be forgotten").
  • Right to restrict processing or object to it.
  • Right to lodge a complaint with the Data Protection Inspectorate.

section9.heading: 9. Cookies
section9.body: We only use essential cookies (login, session, language preference and accessibility settings, such as text size, contrast and animations). No advertising, tracking or analytics cookies are used.

section10.heading: 10. Security
section10.body: We implement technical and organisational measures (HTTPS, two-step authentication: email + one-time verification code, access controls, logging and backups) and regularly update the software and service and apply security patches to protect data against unauthorised access, alteration or destruction.

section11.heading: 11. Changes
section11.body: This privacy policy may be updated. Significant changes will be communicated within the product or via email.

section1.heading: 1. General Provisions
section1.body: These Terms of Use govern the use of the SotsiaalAI platform (operator: SotsiaalAI OÜ; hereinafter the Platform). By using the Platform you confirm that you have read these Terms and agree with them.

section2.heading: 2. Service Description
section2.body: The platform provides AI-powered information support in the social sector. Responses are generated based on the SotsiaalAI knowledge base hosted in Estonia, which brings together reliable sources (for example laws, guidelines, service descriptions and other sector-specific information).

section3.heading: 3. Account and Access
section3.items:
• Keep your account secure and your PIN private.
  • Do not share your account or login credentials with third parties.
  • Keep your contact and payment details up to date.

section4.heading: 4. Permitted Use
section4.items:
• It is prohibited to breach security, distribute malware or send mass requests.
  • It is prohibited to transmit unlawful content or violate the rights of others.

section5.heading: 5. Subscription, Payments and Cancellation
section5.paragraph1: The service is provided on a monthly subscription basis (monthly fee ā‚¬7.99). Payments are processed by Maksekeskus AS via the payment method selected by the user.
section5.paragraph2: The subscription can be managed at any time on the profile page ("Manage subscription"). When cancelled, billing stops from the next billing period.
section5.paragraph3: SotsiaalAI may change pricing and subscription terms by publishing the information on the Platform and notifying active subscribers with reasonable prior notice.

section6.heading: 6. Availability and Maintenance
section6.body: We make reasonable efforts to ensure reliability and continuous availability. Maintenance and updates may temporarily limit the service, but we aim to keep interruptions as short as possible.

section7.heading: 7. Reliability and Accuracy
section7.body: SotsiaalAI aims to provide accurate and up-to-date information. If an error or inaccuracy occurs, we will correct it as soon as possible.

section8.heading: 8. Changes to the Terms
section8.body: The Terms of Use may be updated by publishing a new version on the platform. Users will be informed of significant changes via email or in-product notifications.

section9.heading: 9. Use of AI and Data Processing
section9.paragraph1: SotsiaalAI uses language technology developed by OpenAI (GPT model) to generate answers in the language provided by the user, relying on the SotsiaalAI knowledge base hosted in Estonia.
section9.paragraph2: The OpenAI service is used exclusively for text processing. Conversation content is not used for model training or improvement, and data stored permanently (user account, subscription and conversation summaries) is kept securely on servers operated by SotsiaalAI in Estonia.

section10.heading: 10. Data Processing in Chats and Uploaded Materials
section10.paragraph1: When using the Platform, the user may provide information in several ways ā€” by typing, speaking (dictation) or uploading documents. All data provided in this way is processed solely for the purpose of providing the Platform's service, generating responses and ensuring the functioning of the chat, in accordance with the privacy policy.
section10.paragraph2: A more detailed description of data processing and the user's rights is provided in the privacy policy.
section10.items:
• Text entered in the chat. Text entered by the user is transmitted via a secure connection to SotsiaalAI servers to generate a response. The content of the chat is not stored permanently and is not used for marketing, profiling or training models outside the operation of the Platform's service.
  • Dictation (microphone, speech recognition). When using the dictation feature, a short audio clip is recorded, indicated by a red outline around the microphone button. The audio clip is securely transmitted to the speech recognition service and converted into text, which is shown to the user before sending. Audio files are not stored permanently and are used only to create text and to operate the service.
  • Reading responses aloud. The read-aloud feature uses the text displayed in the chat window to present it to the user as audio output. The technology does not permanently store user-related data and does not process it for any other purpose than enabling the read-aloud functionality.
  • Uploaded documents. The user may upload documents to the Platform (for example PDF, DOC/DOCX, TXT, MD or HTML files) so that the assistant can analyse them within the ongoing chat. Documents are not stored permanently, are used only in the context of the given conversation and are removed from the system when the chat window or page is closed. Content derived from the documents is not used for marketing, profiling or training third-party models.
  • Special categories of data. The user may provide sensitive information in the chat or in documents (for example, information about health, family, violence or socio-economic situation). SotsiaalAI processes such data only for the purpose of generating a response and does not process it for other purposes. To ensure security, SotsiaalAI recommends avoiding entering a personal identification code, exact address and full names of third parties, unless this is strictly necessary for using the service.
  • Processors. Technical processing for dictation, read-aloud and response generation may be carried out via authorised service providers. Such providers process data only in accordance with SotsiaalAI's instructions, under concluded agreements and in compliance with applicable data protection requirements. Where processing involves transferring data outside the European Economic Area, appropriate legal safeguards are used.

section11.heading: 11. Intellectual Property
section11.body:
The Platform's trademarks, design, software, technical solution and other content (except for user-submitted data and public source materials) belong to SotsiaalAI OÜ or its partners.

You may use the Platform and its outputs only for personal purposes or for performing your work duties within a reasonable scope. Without SotsiaalAI's written consent, it is prohibited to systematically copy, distribute or use the Platform or its outputs in a way that exceeds normal use or infringes intellectual property rights.

section12.heading: 12. Termination of the Agreement
section12.body:
You may stop using the Platform at any time, including by deleting your account or cancelling your subscription in accordance with these Terms.

SotsiaalAI may suspend or terminate your access to the Platform if you materially breach the Terms, abuse the service, or jeopardise the Platform's security.

section13.heading: 13. Governing Law and Dispute Resolution
section13.body:
These Terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of Estonia.

Disputes will first be attempted to be resolved by negotiation. If no agreement is reached, the dispute will be resolved by the courts of the Republic of Estonia in accordance with applicable law.

intro.paragraph1: SotsiaalAI is an AI-powered platform designed to provide reliable and easy-to-understand support for both social-sector professionals and people seeking help with everyday social questions.
intro.paragraph2: The platform offers two role-based AI assistants: one for professionals and one for people reaching out with personal matters. Both are built to deliver needs-based assistance - whether it concerns legislation, benefits, services, or work-related situations. The answers draw on trusted sources, simplified explanations, and practical guidance.
intro.paragraph3: The social sector is characterised by heavy workloads, fragmented information, and a complex system landscape - something also confirmed by the OSKA report (2025). Our aim is to bring clarity, simplify daily work, and support both professionals and people seeking help.
intro.paragraph4: The platform is developed and operated by SotsiaalAI OÜ. We are a social enterprise whose goal is not to maximise profit, but to solve a societal challenge and create positive impact. Social entrepreneurship combines business activity with a social mission: we generate revenue, but reinvest it into improving the service, expanding access, and strengthening the social sector.
intro.paragraph5: We continuously enhance the service to ensure a timely and practical experience for everyone.

guide.title: How to use the SotsiaalAI platform?
guide.intro: This guide provides a step-by-step overview for getting started on the SotsiaalAI platform: choose a role, create an account, activate a subscription, sign in, use the chat assistant, and configure language and accessibility.

accessibility.title: 1. Language and accessibility
accessibility.body:
On your first visit the Language and accessibility window opens so you can decide how SotsiaalAI should look and behave. You can open the same window later on the Profile page.

  • Language. Choose the interface language: Estonian, Russian or English.
  • Text size. Pick the size that is easiest to read (small, standard, large or extra large).
  • Contrast. Enable high contrast whenever you need clearer buttons and text.
  • Animations. Select "Reduce motion" if movement distracts you or if your computer is slower ā€” this can make the experience more stable.

home.title: 2. Home page
home.body:
The home page has two role cards - "For social work professionals" and "For people seeking help".

  • If you are not signed in, clicking a role card opens the sign-in view, where you can also choose registration.
  • If you are signed in, clicking a role card takes you to chat.
  • The ABOUT section is on the lower part of the home page and can be reached by scrolling (or by using the home page scroll cue).

register.title: 3. Registration and subscription activation
register.body:
To use the SotsiaalAI chat assistant you need an account and an active subscription.

3.1 Registration

  1. On the home page choose the relevant role card and click Register.
  2. Fill in the registration form:
    • email address
    • PIN code (4-8 digit numeric access code)
  3. Accept the terms of use and the privacy policy.
  4. After submitting the form we send a verification email - open the message and confirm your email address.

3.2 After confirming your email and activating your subscription

After email confirmation you can sign in with your email address and PIN (see chapter "4. Sign-in and navigation by role").

If your subscription is missing or expired, you will see a notice when using chat, and you can activate access on the Subscription page.

If your subscription is active, you can use chat right away.

signin.title: 4. Sign-in and navigation by role
signin.body:
Sign-in starts on the home page, which shows two role cards: "For social work professionals" and "For people seeking help".

  • Click the relevant role card to open the sign-in dialog.
  • Enter your email address and PIN (4-8 digit numeric access code).
  • On the same previously trusted device/browser, the PIN is usually sufficient.
  • If you sign in from a new or unusual environment, we send a 6-digit verification code to your email. Enter the code in the input field.
  • Optionally select "Remember this device for 30 days" so that on this device you will usually only need your PIN next time.
  • If you forgot your PIN, use the Forgot PIN link to set a new PIN by following the instructions.
  • After a successful sign-in the chat view for your role opens. If access is limited (e.g. subscription is missing or expired), a notice and next steps are shown.

chat.title: 5. Chat page
chat.body:
Chat page opens after sign-in. To actively use chat (for example, sending questions or document analysis), your account must have an active subscription. The assistant is role-based - answers and recommendations may differ depending on your role (for example, specialist or help-seeker). Role-specific functions on the chat page may differ slightly, but the core workflow - sending messages, adding documents, and managing chats - is similar for everyone.

Sending a message

  • Type your question in the input field below the chat window and send it with the button or Enter.
  • While the answer is being generated, the send button turns into a stop button, so you can stop streaming and continue typing.

Viewing messages and scrolling

  • If you scroll up, an arrow icon appears near the input field so you can jump to the bottom with one click.

Add a person to a group chat

If you want to include another person in the chat, you can send them an invite. After they accept, you will have a shared room (group chat) where you can chat together.

  • Open the chat page and select "Add person" from the top bar (or open "Rooms" and click "Invite" for a room).
  • If you are creating a new room, enter a room name.
  • Enter the invitee email address.
  • If you enter multiple addresses, separate them with commas.
  • Choose the subscription option: they have their own subscription or I will pay for them.
  • Click "Send invite".
  • The invite appears in the "Invites" list, where you can track its status.
  • Once the person accepts, you can open the group chat from the "Rooms" view or from the same chat page.

Room management

  • In the "Rooms" view you can invite people, leave a room, or delete a room (delete is only available to the room owner).

Document analysis

  • Uploading a document helps the assistant work with your text ā€” for example a longer letter, contract, guide, or application.
  • The assistant can use the document to create a brief summary, clarify complex or legal parts, compare terms against laws or guidelines, help draft replies and applications, and highlight important dates, requirements, and risks.
  • The document is used only within the current conversation and is not stored permanently.

Adding a document

  • The paperclip button opens a file picker and lets you upload documents (up to 50 MB), e.g. PDF, DOC/DOCX, TXT, MD, or HTML.
  • Documents are not stored permanently ā€” they are used only for the current conversation.

After a successful upload, a document window opens where you see:

  • the file name,
  • the "Analyse in chat" toggle to choose whether the assistant may rely on the uploaded document or only on the SotsiaalAI knowledge base when answering,
  • a clarification that opens when you click the question-mark icon,
  • the "Show document" / "Hide document" button to open or hide the document text in the window,
  • the document text (scrollable in the same window) when "Show document" is selected,
  • the "Back to chat" button to jump back to the chat window and input field to continue with questions.

Under the "Upload document" button you see how many document analyses you still have available today.

Sources

  • If an answer uses documents, a "Sources" button appears in the footer to open the list of materials used.

Chat sidebar

  • The "Conversations" button in the quick-actions rail opens a side panel where you can:
  • load previous conversations,
  • start a new conversation,
  • refresh the list,
  • delete conversations you no longer need,
  • use "Load more" to view older conversations.

The active conversation is highlighted in the list and the panel closes automatically when you switch to another conversation.

Profile and accessibility

  • From the chat pageā€™s top-right profile icon you can open the profile page and accessibility settings (see the "Profile and accessibility" chapter).

Warnings

  • If the conversation touches a possible crisis, a red info box appears with instructions for getting urgent help.

profile.title: 6. Profile
profile.body:
The "Profile" page contains your account, subscription and accessibility settings.

  • See your user role.
  • Show subscription: plan, monthly price and validity.
  • To change email enter your current PIN; we send a verification email to the new address.
  • To update PIN enter your current PIN and a new PIN (4-8 digits).
  • Adjust language, text size, contrast and motion preferences.
  • In the profile quick actions menu, you can switch between dark and light layout.

about.title: 7. ABOUT
about.body:
The ABOUT section is on the home page below the role cards. It includes:

  • a short explanation of who the platform is for and how it helps;
  • contact by email: info@sotsiaal.ai;
  • Terms of Use and Privacy Policy;
  • a link to the Platform guide (this guide).

quickstart.title: 8. Quick overview
quickstart.body:
This is a short guide to using SotsiaalAI.

First time

  1. Open sotsiaal.ai
    On your first visit choose language and accessibility (text size, contrast, animations).
  2. Choose a role and create an account
    • On the home page pick the role card ("For social work professionals" or "For people seeking help") and click Register.
    • Enter your email address and a PIN (4-8 digits) and accept the terms and privacy policy.
  3. Confirm email and sign in
    • Open the verification email and confirm your address.
    • Then sign in with email + PIN. If needed, confirm with the 6-digit code sent to your email and optionally select "Remember this device for 30 days" on devices you trust.
  4. Activate subscription
    • If you do not yet have an active subscription, after the first sign-in you will be redirected to the Subscription page.
    • Choose the plan and confirm payment (monthly fee ā‚¬7.99).
  5. Start using the chat assistant
    • On the chat page type your question in the input field and press Enter or click "Send".
    • Use documents and sources as described in section 5 of this guide.

Next visits

  1. Open sotsiaal.ai.
  2. If your device is remembered and you have an active subscription, you can usually sign in with just your PIN.
  3. If something seems off or access is limited, check your subscription status on the Profile page.

subscription.title: Subscription

payment.confirmation_pending: Payment was started. Access is activated after provider confirmation.

active.summary: Your subscription is active. Monthly price: 7.99.
active.cancel_note: You can cancel your subscription at any time on the profile page.

subscription.info: To use SotsiaalAI you need a monthly subscription priced at 7.99. Payments are charged automatically via Maksekeskus with the payment method you choose. The subscription renews each month automatically and you can cancel it at any time from your profile. The service remains active until the end of the paid period.
